queries.query_text: Fix moveAcrossFilesystems in MountManager to delete destination when source deletion fails after successful copy

solve_notes: Split `moveAcrossFilesystems` into two separate try/catch blocks. The first covers the copy operation; the second covers the delete. When delete fails, we attempt to clean up the already-created destination before rethrowing `UnableToMoveFile`, preventing orphaned destination files.
task_text:
Fix MountManager.moveAcrossFilesystems leaving orphaned destination on partial failure

In `src/MountManager.php`, `moveAcrossFilesystems` performs a move
by calling `$this->copy()` followed by `$this->delete()`. If `copy`
succeeds but `delete` fails (e.g., the source adapter throws
`UnableToDeleteFile`), the method catches the exception and rethrows
it as `UnableToMoveFile`, but makes no attempt to remove the
already-created destination file. The caller receives an error yet
the destination file now exists as an orphan. Fix
`moveAcrossFilesystems` to detect this partial-failure case — when
`delete` throws after a successful `copy` — and attempt to delete the
destination file before rethrowing `UnableToMoveFile`, so that a
failed cross-mount move does not leave orphaned files behind.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: copyAcrossFilesystem
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: seeing how copyAcrossFilesystem is structured helps an agent understand the cleanup pattern available for the delete-failure scenario

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the edited method and the exception factory. An AI agent would also look at move() to understand the dispatch logic and at copyAcrossFilesystem to see whether there's an existing cleanup pattern to follow. Without these, the agent might edit move() directly or implement cleanup differently than the existing idioms.
